paper_title: Pollen and Meteorological Data Analysis in Linz

methods.description: Analysis of serial pollen counts for alder, hazel, birch, and grass to determine the pollen season duration and its phenological start and end.

steps.description: Data of serial pollen counts for alder, hazel, birch and grass, having been continuously recorded since 1993, were analyzed.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch pollen count data from 1993 onwards

steps.description: Seasonal data sets with gaps >6 days were omitted.
steps.searchable_summary: filter out seasonal data sets with gaps greater than 6 days

steps.description: Begin and end of pollen seasons-pollination (PSB-PSE)-were defined phenologically as first and last days of a year, reaching 10 pollen/m3 (PC10) by alder, hazel and birch, and 5 pollen/m3 (PC5) by grass species, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: define pollen season start and end based on pollen concentration thresholds
outputs: pollen season start and end dates

steps.description: The time in-between was considered the pollen season or its duration.
inputs: pollen season start and end dates

methods.description: Analysis of meteorological data to derive temperature metrics and study their influence on the pollen season.

steps.description: The Zentralanstalt für Meteorologie und Geodynamik (ZAMG Wien-Salzburg) provided meteorological data from 1993-2018 in form of hourly and daily temperatures (°C), precipitation (PREC in mm = L/m2) and relative humidity (RH in %).
steps.searchable_summary: fetch meteorological data from ZAMG for 1993-2018

steps.description: From hourly values, the highest temperature (HTD), the mean temperature (MTD) and the lowest temperature (LTD) of a day were derived.
steps.searchable_summary: derive daily temperature metrics from hourly data

steps.description: The mean daily warmth (MDW) was defined as integral of 1/24 hourly temperatures >0°C/24 h and the cumulated one as daily MDW summed up from January 1 (MDWcumul).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean daily warmth and its cumulative value

steps.description: The mean daily frost (MDF) was regarded as the integral of 1/24 hourly temperatures <0°C/24 h. Thus frost and cumulated frost (MDFcumul, summed up beginning at January 1) reflect temperatures <0°C.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean daily frost and its cumulative value

steps.description: For the meteorologically driven start of the pollen season, especially for early flowering plants, both, the cumulated mean daily warmth (MDWcumul) and a distinct threshold of HTD were considered necessary.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze meteorological influence on pollen season start

steps.description: The influence of frost was studied by using the MDFcumul.
steps.searchable_summary: study frost influence on pollen season using MDFcumul
